dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 3 responding for chest pain.
Hi, I'm Alex, an EMT with the rescue squad. What made you call 911 today?
I have chest pain, it started about 10 minutes ago while I was driving from Walmart.
Can you tell me your name, please?
My name is Jennifer.
What county are we in, Jennifer?
I think it's [CITY].
What is today's date?
December the 5th, I think.
What happened today after you got in the car and started driving back from Walmart, Jennifer?
I got this sharp chest pain, but now I feel better and I think it was just anxiety again.
Eyes are open spontaneously, equal and reactive bilaterally to light.
That's good to hear, Jennifer. You seem alert and oriented to person, place, and time. Would you say you're aware of why you're here?
Yes. I know I was experiencing chest pain and needed help.
And are you alert and oriented right now-knowing your name, where you are, and what the date is?
Yes, I'm oriented to all that.
You seem to be breathing and speaking clearly. Are there any issues with your airway?
No, I'm fine.
I hear and see that your breaths are at an average rate and depth. Have you experienced any shortness of breath?
No, not at all.
Pulse is 70, regular. Blood pressure is 109 over 71, and SpO2 is 98% on room air.
When did the chest pain start exactly? Did you do anything that might have caused it? And have you noticed any changes since you first felt it?
I was halfway home from Walmart when I started feeling the chest pain. It hasn't really changed; it's just not as sharp now, and I feel better overall.

Jennifer, can you tell me if you're allergic to any medications or have you used or reacted to any medication in the past that you remember? Just want to double-check before doing any procedure, okay?
I'm allergic to codeine and guaifenesin, but those are the only ones I've had a reaction to in the past. Otherwise, I don't take allergy meds usually because they make me feel worse.

What medications are you currently taking on a regular schedule, Jennifer? Is there anything you've taken right before the chest pain began? Did you take anything recently for pain or anxiety or depression?
I take methadone and clonidine. That's it. I haven't taken anything new recently, and I haven't taken any over-the-counter meds today.

On your record, I see that you have depression, hepatitis C, hypertension, anxiety, and migraines. Is that correct, Jennifer? How has it felt now with the chest pain that you had today? Has your anxiety been worse lately, or are things under control for now?

Yes, that's correct. I do have a history of all that. I think my anxiety has been worse lately actually, but I've been trying to manage it through relaxation, walking, and things like that. I'm not feeling down now.
You're 43, correct? You weigh 73 kilograms. Is that still correct for you?
Yeah, I've been pretty consistent in weight, so yeah that's right.
On a scale from 0 to 10, with 10 being the worst pain you've ever had in your life, how bad would you describe this chest pain?
10, but I've felt that pain before with my migraines. In general, I think a 5, maybe a 6 now with it fading off would be more accurate.
And what was happening just before the chest pain? In your words, what were you doing that might have triggered it today?
I can't say that anything was happening at all when it started. I was just driving home from Walmart.
How would you describe the chest pain? Is it sharp, burning, or stabbing, or more like a pressure or a dull ache?
The chest pain was really sharp and stabbing. It's really hard to describe but once it's gone, it doesn't feel the same.
When you had the pain, did it spread anywhere else in your body besides your chest?
It did not go anywhere else. It just stayed in the center of my chest, never spreading to anything else.
So, the worst of it was a 5 to 6 on the 10 scale with sharp stabbing. Any nausea or dizziness or vomiting with it?
Nope, no nausea, no dizziness, no projectile vomiting. Just the chest pain, and I didn't pass out or feel like I was shaking.
How long did the chest pain last?
Maybe around 10 minutes, just until it went away on its own.
Pulse is 70.
Resp rate is 18.
Blood pressure is 109 over 71.
SpO2 is 98%.
Glucose could not be tested and is unrecorded due to patient declining or protocol constraints.
EKG shows sinus rhythm.
You appear alert and oriented to person, place, time, and event. Is that accurate, Jennifer?
Yes. I understand what happened and why I called 911.
I'm seeing no signs of rashes, petechiae, or any visible injuries.
Eyes are reactive, head and neck exams show no findings.
Breath sounds are equal and non-radiating.
I see you walking freely, no deformities or angulation observed.
No tenderness on palpation in abdomen.
Let's start with a 4-lead monitor.
4-lead monitoring successfully applied.
Starting 12-lead EKG monitoring now.
12-lead EKG successfully obtained. Interpretation shows borderline findings.
Field impression is anxiety disorder. No interventions needed.
Medical - Chest Pain - Cardiac Suspected (Protocol 2-1) activated.
Jennifer, the evaluation we've done shows the pain has subsided and the vital signs are stable. Still, based on protocol, we strongly consider transporting you to the hospital.
No. I'm okay with this.
Are you sure?
We need to document your refusal. The risks have been explained to you.
Yes, I understand and am choosing to refuse transport.
Let's monitor your condition and call back if needed.
You're handling this well. Call if anything changes.
You're doing great. We're always here if you need us.
Feel free to reach out if symptoms return.
I'll be around for support if anything happens.
Otherwise, we are wrapping up the call.
Momentary ambulatory assessment confirmed patient is standing and walking freely.
Pulse and BP remain stable.
SpO2 is within normal limits.
You're doing great, Jennifer.
Any questions before we finish?
